message:
type: CLAIM
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
summary: Adding relevance_scores param to NDCG.compute for graded relevance support

type: CLAIM
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
summary: Fixing NDCG IDCG calculation to use len(expected_ids) instead of min(retrieved,expected)

type: INTENT
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
summary: My change is scoped to NDCG.compute lines ~380-386: replacing ideal_length=min(len(retrieved_ids),len(expected_ids)) with len(expected_ids) for IDCG. Please rebase your relevance_scores addition on top of this so we don't clobber each other; I'll ping when done.

type: ANSWER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
summary: Ack: I already added relevance_scores param to NDCG.compute (dcg uses get_relevance(docid), idcg uses sorted ideal_relevances[i-1], ideal_length still min(retrieved,expected)). Will rebase ideal_length line onto your len(expected_ids) fix once you ping done.

type: STATUS
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
summary: Done: IDCG now iterates range(1, len(expected_ids)+1) instead of min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)); removed the ideal_length variable. Safe to rebase your ideal_length line onto len(expected_ids).
